data.title: Backblaze reports strong Q2 2025 results: 29% B2 cloud storage revenue growth, 16% total revenue growth.
data.description: In an announcement from SAN MATEO, California, Backblaze, Inc. (Nasdaq: BLZE) revealed its financial performance for the second quarter of 2025. The company reported a 29% increase in revenue specifically in B2 Cloud Storage and a 16% revenue growth overall during Q2. Notably, there was a 30% rise year over year in customers contributing over $50,000 in ARR, an increase from the previously reported 53%. This data reflects the company's up-market momentum.

data.title: Papa John's stock rose premarket after reporting Q2 earnings of 41 cents per share, $529.166M in sales (+4.2%).
data.description: Papa John’s International, Inc. saw a rise in its shares trading higher before market hours on Thursday. The company posted adjusted earnings per share for the second quarter at 41 cents, surpassing the expected 34 cents. Quarterly sales stood at $529.166 million, marking a 4.2% year-over-year increase, surpassing analyst projections of $515.601 million. President and CEO Todd Penegor commented, "We returned to comparable sales growth in North America and achieved strong sales growth internationally, driven by transaction gains as we win more customer visits with a focus on our core pizza business." The revenue boost was mainly due to a $20.3 million surge in Commissary revenues, reflecting higher volumes and pricing during the quarter.

data.title: Employers face rising economic uncertainty and return-to-office mandates as per HireRight’s 2025 Global Benchmark Report.
data.description: A recent report by HireRight reveals unexpected findings on workforce recruitment and retention issues. The 2025 Global Benchmark Report from the company based in Nashville presents crucial insights regarding this industry.

data.title: DraftKings Q2 FY2025 Earnings: Revenue grew 37% yoy to $1.5B, adjusted EBITDA over $300M, maintaining guidance for FY2025. 5 key takeaways discussed. More details at Benzinga.com.
data.description: DraftKings Inc (NASDAQ: DKNG) reported record revenue and adjusted EBITDA in the second quarter, with revenue up 37% year over year to $1.51 billion and adjusted EBITDA over $300 million. Sportsbook net revenue increased 45% year over year to $11.5 billion, with a record net revenue margin of 8.7%. Live betting handle was up 16% year over year, and the company is maintaining its fiscal year 2025 revenue guidance of $6.2-6.4 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $800-900 million. DraftKings is focusing on profitability and efficiency initiatives while also exploring opportunities in prediction markets, aiming to maximize shareholder returns over the long term. They have repurchased 6.5 million shares and continue to invest in organic growth initiatives. CEO Jason Robins highlighted the company's strong momentum and innovative sportsbook product, positioning them for success in upcoming NFL and NBA seasons. They are also monitoring potential impact from mobile sports betting launches in Missouri and changes in tax rates in New Jersey, Louisiana, and Illinois. These developments indicate their strategic focus on driving growth and maximizing shareholder value.

data.title: Latin America Non-Lethal Weapons Market projected to reach US$652.65 million by 2033 from US$407.51 million in 2024, at a CAGR of 5.37% from 2025.
data.description: A report titled "Latin America Non-Lethal Weapons Market Share Analysis and Size - Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The Latin America Non-Lethal Weapons Market is projected to grow from US$ 407.51 million in 2024 to US$ 652.65 million by 2033, reflecting a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 5.37% from 2025 to 2033. The market's expansion is primarily fueled by the increasing demand for effective crowd control techniques in the region.

data.title: Ripple acquires Rail for $200M for stablecoin payments, to integrate RLUSD and expand globally.
data.description: Ripple recently completed the acquisition of Rails to expand its offerings in stablecoin payment services. This move will involve the integration of RLUSD, banking partners, and compliance tools in various international markets. This strategic decision will allow Ripple to broaden its reach and enhance its services for users around the world.
